---
title: "UNIONS-3500 Weak Lensing:<br>$B$-mode validation for cosmic shear"
occasion: Euclid Weak Lensing SWG telecon
description: "UNIONS-3500 B-mode validation for cosmic shear"
image: images/pure_eb_data_vector.png
author:
    name: Cail Daley
    affiliation: "CosmoStat, CEA Paris-Saclay  <br> <br> [on behalf of the UNIONS weak lensing team]{style=font-size:1.3em}"
date: April 27, 2026
date-format: long

format:
    revealjs:
        width:  1920
        height: 1080
        theme: [default, custom.scss]
        slide-number: true
        fig-align: center
        menu: false
        include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
        footer: Cail Daley | Euclid WL SWG telecon | April 27, 2026
        history: false
        hash: true
        pdf-separate-fragments: true
        pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
        template-partials:
            - title-slide.html
        center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
    method: mathjax
    url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## UNIONS is the first wide-field lensing survey in the northern sky

::: {.r-stack}
::: {.fragment .fade-out fragment-index=0 style="margin-right:-4%;margin-left:-4%"}
![](images/des_y6_footprints.png){width=108% fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.fragment fragment-index=0 style="margin-right:-4%;margin-left:-4%"}
![](images/des_y6_footprints_unions.png){width=108% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="position:absolute;bottom:8px;right:6%;font-size:0.55em;opacity:0.7"}
Credit: DES Collaboration
:::
:::
:::

::: notes
Existing Stage-III cosmic shear surveys all live in the southern sky and overlap each other. UNIONS fills the north — zero overlap with DES, KiDS, or HSC. Different instrument, different Galactic foreground, different on-sky systematics. The only Stage-III survey that doesn't share sky with any of the others.
:::


## UNIONS-3500 at a glance

::: {style="margin-top:100px;font-size:1.05em"}
| | |
|:-|:-|
| **Imaging** | CFHT MegaCam $r$-band; $r_\mathrm{median} = 0.7''$ |
| **Effective area** | $2894~\mathrm{deg}^2$ after masking<br>(target $6250~\mathrm{deg}^2$) |
| **Source density** | $n_\mathrm{eff} \approx 5.0~\mathrm{arcmin}^{-2}$ |
| **Shape noise** | $\sigma_e \approx 0.27$ per component |
| **Shape measurement** | `ShapePipe` + metacalibration |
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px;text-align:center;font-size:0.95em;opacity:0.85"}
Non-tomographic in this release; multi-bin calibration in preparation.
:::


## Paper II validates B modes for the UNIONS-3500 release

::: {style="margin-top:70px"}
- **Paper I** — Catalogues (Hervas-Peters et al.)
- **Paper II** — $B$-mode validation (this work)
- **Paper III** — Configuration-space cosmology (Goh et al.)
- **Paper IV** — Harmonic-space cosmology (Guerrini et al.)
- **Paper V** — Image simulations (Hervas-Peters et al.)
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px;text-align:center"}
Paper II sets the scale cuts adopted in Papers III and IV.
:::


## Cosmic shear produces $E$ modes; $B$ modes flag systematics

::: {style="margin-top:50px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Scalar potential → curl-free shear field → only $E$ modes to leading order
- Higher-order effects produce $B$ modes far below current sensitivity
- At Stage-III, **$B$ modes flag residual systematics**
- Null $B$ modes do not rule out contamination
:::

:::::::::::::: {.columns style="margin-top:30px"}
::: {.column .center style="width:50%;text-align:center"}
![](images/emode_pattern.jpg){width=78%}

::: {style="font-size:0.85em;margin-top:-4px"}
$E$ modes (gradient-like)
:::
:::
::: {.column .center style="width:50%;text-align:center"}
![](images/bmode_pattern.jpg){width=78%}

::: {style="font-size:0.85em;margin-top:-4px"}
$B$ modes (curl-like)
:::
:::
::::::::::::::


## Stage-III B-mode failures trace<br>detector, PSF, and astrometric effects

::: {style="margin-top:70px;font-size:1.05em"}
- **Repeating additive shear bias** — CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV [[Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353)]
- **PSF higher-moment leakage** — HSC-Y3 [[Zhang+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03257)]
- **Astrometric residuals** — KiDS-Legacy [[Wright+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.19441)]
- **`HybridEB` flips DES-Y3, KiDS, HSC-Y3 from pass to fail** [[Jefferson+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.03964)]
    - …yet DES-Y6 itself adopts `HybridEB` and passes — only one appendix figure and two paragraphs of discussion [[DES-Y6](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.10065)]
:::

::: {style="margin-top:60px;text-align:center;font-size:0.95em;opacity:0.85"}
Each estimator weights angular scales differently; the same data can pass one and fail another.
:::


## Three Stage-III surveys see the same oscillatory $B$-mode pattern

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:140px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Three surveys, three pipelines
- $B_n$ panels (right): same coherent oscillation
- Survey $p$-values differ, the **shape** doesn't
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/asgari19_allsurveys_EnBn.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs $E_n$ / $B_n$ — DES-SV, KiDS-450, CFHTLenS. From [Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353).
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
The B-mode panels on the right tell the story: a non-random oscillating residual that looks similar across DES-SV, KiDS-450 and CFHTLenS. The p-values disagree because covariances and areas differ — but the shape is shared. This is the signature we'll see in UNIONS too.
:::


## Mock systematics in COSEBIs each leave a distinct fingerprint

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:140px;font-size:1.0em"}
- One systematic, one signature
- **Regular pattern** (black): chaotic high-$n$ oscillations — matches the surveys' data
- High-$n$ carries the fingerprint
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/asgari19_mocksys_EnBn.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs response to four mock systematics on KiDS-450. From [Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353).
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
"Regular pattern" is a c-term that repeats on focal-plane scales — the CCD-scale repeating additive bias mechanism. Compare the black stars to the previous slide's right column: same kind of signature. So the field has both the empirical pattern across surveys and a candidate mechanism that reproduces it in mocks. Hold this in mind when we look at the UNIONS COSEBIs.
:::


## We use three $E$/$B$-separable statistics<br>with different filter supports

::: {style="margin-top:50px;font-size:1.05em"}
- **Pure-mode correlation functions** $\xi_\pm^{\mathrm{E/B}}(\theta)$ &nbsp; [[Schneider+22](https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.09774)]
    - integral transforms of $\xi_\pm$; deproject ambiguous modes
- **COSEBIs** $E_n, B_n$ &nbsp; [[Schneider+10](https://arxiv.org/abs/1002.2136)]
    - discrete orthogonal modes on $[\theta_\mathrm{min}, \theta_\mathrm{max}]$; deproject ambiguous modes
- **Catalog-based $C_\ell^{EE}, C_\ell^{BB}$** &nbsp; [[Wolz+25](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21013)]
    - spin-2 SHT at source positions; residual $E{\to}B$ leakage absorbed into $C_\ell^{BB}$
:::

::: {style="margin-top:50px;font-size:0.95em;text-align:center;opacity:0.85"}
Gaussian-only covariance (CosmoCov + NaMaster iNKA), validated on 350 GLASS mocks [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)] — PTEs are conservative.
:::


## Pure-mode $B$ modes show low-level structure<br>outside the adopted cuts

![](images/pure_eb_data_vector.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}


## COSEBIs and $C_\ell^{BB}$ also pass within the adopted range

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

::: {style="margin-top:120px;font-size:1.0em"}
- **COSEBIs** $B_n$
    - $>4\sigma$ first-mode excess at full range
    - Suppressed by the cuts; first six modes carry the cosmology
- **Harmonic-space** $C_\ell^{BB}$
    - Low-level offset across $\ell$
    - Passes within the adopted range $\ell\in[300,1600]$
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

![](images/cosebis_data_vector.png){width=98% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-4px;margin-bottom:6px"}
Full range (orange), fiducial cuts (blue).
:::

![](images/cl_data_vector.png){width=98% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;text-align:center;margin-top:-4px"}
$C_\ell^{BB}/\sigma$ top, $C_\ell^{EB}/\sigma$ bottom.
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Only the fiducial catalog passes all three statistics

:::::::::::::: {.columns style="margin-top:40px;font-size:0.95em;text-align:center"}
::: {.column width="25%"}
**initial**<br>loose size cut
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
**fiducial**<br>$r_\mathrm{gal}/r_\mathrm{psf} > 0.707$
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
**masked**<br>fiducial + star-halo masks
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
**relaxed-flags**<br>$+23\%$ source density
:::
::::::::::::::

![](images/pte_table_appendix_paper3.png){width=92% fig-align="center" style="margin-top:40px"}

::: {style="margin-top:14px;text-align:center;font-size:0.85em;opacity:0.85"}
**Bold** entries fail the null test (PTE $< 0.05$).
:::


## The adopted cuts sit in broad PTE-pass regions

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:180px;font-size:0.95em"}
- $\chi^2$ vs. zero per $(\theta_\mathrm{min},\theta_\mathrm{max})$ cut
- Adopted: $\theta\in[12',83']$, $\ell\in[300,1600]$
- Moving cuts several bins either way doesn't change the outcome
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/pte_cosebis_talk.png){width=96% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.72em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs PTE vs. lower/upper scale cut. Square: adopted cuts.
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Filter functions weight scales differently —<br>cuts don't map between estimators

![](images/harmonic_config_cosebis_full.png){width=72% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.78em;text-align:center;margin-top:-10px"}
$E_n$ (top), $B_n/\sigma_n$ (bottom), from $\xi_\pm$ (circles) and $C_\ell$ (squares).
:::

::: {style="margin-top:30px;font-size:1.0em"}
- COSEBIs computed from $\xi_\pm$ and from $C_\ell$ via $W_n(\ell)$ agree across all 20 modes
- $C_\ell^{BB}$ itself passes — but COSEBIs from the same $C_\ell$ do not
- COSEBI filters concentrate sensitivity where the contamination sits
:::

::: notes
The disagreement is not a matter of harmonic versus real space — both COSEBI paths fail together while $C_\ell^{BB}$ passes. Filter functions, not basis, drive what each estimator sees.
:::


## Our oscillatory COSEBIs match a familiar<br>Stage-III B-mode signature

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:54%"}

::: {style="margin-top:30px;font-size:0.98em"}
- The same oscillatory $B_n$ pattern recurs in CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV [[Asgari+19](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353)]
- Asgari+19 model it as a spatially varying $c$-term fixed in focal-plane coordinates (right) — but the recurrence across detectors hints at something more general
- A localized-in-$\theta$ contamination, viewed through orthogonal COSEBI filters, naturally produces oscillations in $B_n$
- Either way, our $12'$ lower cut excludes the angular range where the pattern lives
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:46%"}

::: {style="margin-top:-10px"}
![](images/asgari19_regular_pattern.png){width=72% fig-align="center"}
:::

::: {style="font-size:0.78em;text-align:center;margin-top:-2px"}
Repeating focal-plane $c$-term mock, $5\times$ amplified. Credit: [Asgari et al. 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.02353).
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
The honest framing: we don't know the source from first principles. The pattern recurs across surveys with different detectors (CFHTLenS used MegaCam, but DES-SV did not), so a strict detector-scale story doesn't fit. What does fit is that any contamination localized at small θ projects through COSEBIs' orthogonal filters into oscillations at high n. Paper V identifies one such source in our own pipeline (next slide).
:::


## Paper V identifies an additional pipeline-level source at CCD scales

::: {style="margin-top:110px;font-size:1.05em"}
- Multi-exposure fitting centers on each exposure's pixel grid independently, rather than the WCS-anchored tile-level detection
- Pixel offsets between exposures in overlapping CCDs imprint a preferred orientation → **coherent additive shear at CCD scale**
- Multiplicative bias absorbs part of it; the adopted cuts remove the rest
:::

::: notes
So we have two candidate sources for the CCD-scale repeating additive bias: the detector-level c-term mechanism identified by Asgari+19 across multiple Stage-III surveys, and this pipeline-level effect from improper exposure-offset propagation that Paper V has now identified. The adopted cuts remove both without needing to model either.
:::


## We couldn't localize the source,<br>so we cut where all three statistics pass

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:1.05em"}
- We detected an oscillatory $B$-mode pattern in COSEBIs at the full angular range
- We have not yet localized the source from first principles — two candidate mechanisms remain on the table
- We adopted $\theta_\mathrm{min}=12'$, $\ell_\mathrm{max}=1600$, where pure-mode, COSEBIs, and $C_\ell$ all pass
- We cross-checked single-framework failures across the other two — only the fiducial catalog passes in every representation
- We treated $B$ modes as a diagnostic, not a bias to subtract: the cuts that remove $B$-mode contamination remove its $E$-mode counterpart too, with no $E/B$-ratio model
:::


## Summary

<ul style="margin-top:80px;font-size:1.05em">
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">UNIONS-3500 $B$ modes pass at the adopted cuts across all three statistics</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">At full range, our COSEBIs show an oscillatory pattern matching the CFHTLenS-era repeating additive bias (same MegaCam)</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.2em">Only the fiducial (tightened-size-cut) catalog passes in every representation</li>
<li>Filter functions, not basis, drove which estimators flagged the contamination</li>
</ul>

::: {style="margin-top:60px;text-align:center"}
Paper II on arXiv: [arxiv.org/abs/2604.03227](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.03227)<br>[cail.daley@cea.fr]{style="font-size:0.9em"}
:::


## Backup {background-color="black" style="color:white" visibility="uncounted"}


## Ambiguous modes arise on any finite interval {visibility="uncounted"}

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:0.98em"}
On a finite angular interval, shear patterns with functional forms
$$\xi_+^{\mathrm{amb}}(\theta) = a + b\theta^2, \quad \xi_-^{\mathrm{amb}}(\theta) = c\theta^{-2} + d\theta^{-4}$$
cannot be assigned pure $E$- or $B$-mode origin.

<br>

Pure-mode and COSEBI filters deproject these modes; the catalog-based $C_\ell$ estimator absorbs residual mask-induced leakage into $C_\ell^{BB}$.
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:50%"}
![](images/emode_pattern.jpg){width=75%}
![](images/bmode_pattern.jpg){width=75%}
:::
::::::::::::::


## Pure-mode $B$ modes: data vectors across catalog versions {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/pure_eb_versions.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.82em;text-align:center;margin-top:6px"}
$\xi_+$ (left), $\xi_-$ (right); $E$ (top), $B/\sigma$ (bottom). Initial (red), fiducial size cuts (gold), $+$masking (teal), relaxed flags (purple). Shaded: outside adopted range.
:::


## COSEBIs $B$ modes: data vectors across catalog versions {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/cosebis_bmode_stacked.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.82em;text-align:center;margin-top:6px"}
$B_n$ stacked across catalog versions. Same colors: initial (red), fiducial (gold), masking (teal), relaxed flags (purple).
:::


## $C_\ell^{BB}$: data vectors across catalog versions {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/cl_versions.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.82em;text-align:center;margin-top:6px"}
$C_\ell^{BB}/\sigma$ across catalog versions. Same colors as above.
:::


## PTE maps across all catalog versions and config-space statistics {visibility="uncounted"}

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:42%"}

![](images/config_space_pte_composite_appendix.png){fig-align="center" height="950"}

:::
::: {.column style="width:58%"}

::: {style="margin-top:240px;font-size:1.0em"}
- **Rows** — catalog versions:<br>initial · fiducial · $+$masking · relaxed flags
- **Columns** — statistics: $\xi_+^B$, $\xi_-^B$, COSEBI $B_n$
- **Color** — PTE (blue $\le 0.05$ fail, red $\ge 0.95$)
- **Black squares** — adopted cuts
- The fiducial row is the only one where the adopted-cut square sits in a pass region across all three statistics
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Covariance: semi-analytic + MC propagation {visibility="uncounted"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- **Harmonic-space** ($C_\ell^{BB}$, $C_\ell^{EB}$): Gaussian covariance from NaMaster's iNKA framework
- **Configuration-space** (pure-mode, COSEBIs): `CosmoCov` covariance of $\xi_\pm$, propagated through the filter transforms
- COSEBIs: linear transformation $\mathbf{C}_{B_n B_m} = \mathbf{T}^{\mathsf{T}} \mathbf{C}_{\xi} \mathbf{T}$
- Pure-mode: 2000 MC realizations of $\xi_\pm$, empirical covariance of the transformed data vector
- All $B$-mode null tests use Gaussian-only covariance (conservative: no trispectrum, no super-sample terms)
- Validated on 350 GLASS mocks [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)]: all three statistics recover $B$ modes consistent with zero
:::


## COSEBI mock validation {visibility="uncounted"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- 350 lognormal GLASS catalogs [[Tessore+23](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.01942)] at Planck 2018 cosmology, UNIONS footprint + galaxy positions + weights
- No PSF-related systematics — checks that the estimators don't generate spurious $B$ modes
- COSEBIs: mean $B_n$ biased by $< 0.3\,\sigma$ per mode
- Scatter comparable to fiducial error bars — uncertainty estimates of the right order
:::
